outcome_contract.why: The walkthrough gate fixed a planning-quality blind spot, but it made the markdown artifacts larger. If the handoff packets keep omitting those new process sections, downstream stages will reopen full upstream markdown and lose much of the deterministic token-efficiency gain.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: DO workflow packets carry compact walkthrough/process rows for fact-find, analysis, and plan, and analysis has a deterministic validator so packet-first progressive disclosure remains real after the walkthrough gate.

engineering_coverage.applicable: Downstream stages still reopen full markdown for process-changing work
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Packet-first stays truthful because the new process rows are carried in packets

engineering_coverage.applicable: No new proof artifacts beyond prose
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Generated packets and validator outputs provide deterministic evidence

engineering_coverage.applicable: Analysis remains the only planning-stage artifact without a standalone validator
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: New validator plus existing engineering-coverage gate make analysis fail-closed on missing sections

engineering_coverage.applicable: Packet contract stays outdated relative to the new walkthrough gate
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Contract and generator stay aligned with the actual workflow sections

engineering_coverage.applicable: Walkthrough gate increases markdown size without matching packet lift
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Process rows stay bounded and reduce needless markdown reopening

engineering_coverage.applicable: No new change, but current mismatch persists
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Additive packet/validator rollout with markdown fallback preserved

stage_payload.decision_summary: The walkthrough gate is now required in fact-find, analysis, and plan, but those sections still live only in markdown. That weakens the existing packet-first progressive-disclosure contract because downstream stages must often reopen full upstream artifacts for the very process rows the new gate was meant to make explicit. The decision is how to restore deterministic packet-first handoff without overbuilding a second artifact system.
goals: Keep the walkthrough gate mandatory for process-changing work
Lift the new process rows into deterministic packet fields
Add a real deterministic validator for analysis so `## End-State Operating Model` is enforced by tooling, not only by skill prose
non_goals: Building a new runtime handoff/session system
Measuring every markdown reopen cause in this increment
constraints: The packet fields must stay compact and source-derived
Packet-first must remain packet-first, not packet-only
Analysis validation must align with the sections already required by the skill/template
assumptions: The table structure in the new process sections is stable enough to extract deterministically
`packages/skill-runner` is still the right home for the validator and packet logic
A small workflow-doc update is enough to surface the new validator path

evaluation_criteria.Why it matters: Process rows must be extracted from source artifacts, not resummarized
Downstream stages should rely on packets first for process-changing work
One packet contract should own the new fields
Analysis should not remain the only DO planning-stage artifact without a standalone validator
Existing markdown fallback behavior must remain intact

options_considered.Description: Keep walkthrough sections in markdown only and rely on critique + agent discipline
options_considered.Downside: Packet-first remains incomplete for process-changing work; analysis validator gap stays open
options_considered.Key risks: Workflow claims drift from real behavior

options_considered.Description: Extend packets with compact process rows and add a deterministic analysis validator in the same increment
options_considered.Upside: Restores packet-first handoff for the new sections and closes the analysis gating gap together
options_considered.Downside: Touches contract, generator, validator, and workflow docs
options_considered.Key risks: Small risk of packet-field bloat if extraction is not tightly bounded

options_considered.Description: Measure reopen behavior in telemetry first, then revisit packet/validator changes later
options_considered.Downside: Leaves the current workflow inconsistency in place and does not solve the immediate gap

chosen_approach.Why this wins: It is the smallest coherent change that keeps the walkthrough gate and the packet-first contract aligned. The new sections are already deterministic tables, so lifting them into packet fields is cheap and robust. Adding `validate-analysis.sh` in the same increment closes the only remaining planning-stage validator gap and keeps the workflow symmetric.
chosen_approach.What it depends on: A packet contract update, generator extraction changes, a new analysis validator + wrappers, and small workflow-doc/skill updates.

rejected_approaches: Option A was rejected because it turns packet-first into a soft preference whenever process topology matters.
Option C was rejected because it would add more measurement while knowingly leaving a real workflow/documentation mismatch in place.

planning_focus: Extend packet contract + generator with compact walkthrough/process-row fields for all three upstream stages
Add deterministic analysis validator with the same pattern as fact-find/plan
Update the analysis skill and feature workflow guide to require the new validator
Persist packet sidecars and validation evidence for this slug
validation_implications: `scripts/validate-fact-find.sh` must pass for the new fact-find
New `scripts/validate-analysis.sh` must pass for the new analysis
`scripts/validate-plan.sh` and `scripts/validate-engineering-coverage.sh` must pass for the new plan
`scripts/generate-stage-handoff-packet.sh` must emit all three packet sidecars with the new process-row fields present
sequencing_constraints: Contract + generator before packet generation proof
Analysis validator before updating the analysis skill guidance
New slug packets and build record only after validators pass
risks_to_carry_forward: Packet rows must stay table-derived rather than copying whole section prose
Workflow docs must stay aligned with the actual validator names and order

end_state_process_areas.Delivered step-by-step end state: Analysis loads `fact-find.packet.json`, reads compact trigger/end-condition/process-area rows there first, and reopens `fact-find.md` only if evidence detail is insufficient
end_state_process_areas.Risks / seams to carry into planning: Row extraction must stay bounded to the table/named fields

end_state_process_areas.Delivered step-by-step end state: Plan loads `analysis.packet.json`, reads compact end-state rows first, and reopens `analysis.md` only when path-specific rationale needs verification
end_state_process_areas.Risks / seams to carry into planning: Some option nuance still lives only in markdown

end_state_process_areas.Delivered step-by-step end state: Build loads `plan.packet.json`, sees task briefs plus compact delivered-process rows, then escalates to the specific task block in `plan.md` for execution
end_state_process_areas.What remains unchanged: Build still reads the full task block before editing/executing
end_state_process_areas.Risks / seams to carry into planning: Packet-first must not be misread as packet-only

end_state_process_areas.Delivered step-by-step end state: Analysis runs `validate-analysis.sh` first, then `validate-engineering-coverage.sh` when the track is `code or mixed`, then emits `analysis.packet.json`
end_state_process_areas.What remains unchanged: Engineering-coverage validation remains required for code/mixed work
end_state_process_areas.Risks / seams to carry into planning: Historical analyses are not auto-revalidated until touched
